# Transcrição: 10 Custom Audience Customer List

- **Fonte:** 10 Custom Audience Customer List
- **Duração:** 02:00
- **Idioma:** en
- **Modelo:** small

---

## [00:00 - 02:00]

To create an audience of an email list, you'll need to click on customer file. Now this one's pretty straightforward. You can either import straight from Mailchimp, so if you click on this, it's going to ask you for Mailchimp credentials. You can log in through this. Or you can copy and paste or upload a CSV file of your list. Now Facebook is able to match your list on 15 identifiers as you'll see here. So email, phone numbers, first name, date of birth, year of birth, gender, age. You don't need all of those. The more you have, the closer it will be able to match everyone on that list. Typically what you'll see is the match rate will be about 70%. So if you upload a list of 100 people, Facebook will be able to match about 70 of those people. The second option that you have is you can copy and paste your list. So you can simply open your own database file and then paste them in here. And Facebook is going to ask you to then select the name, the email, or any other identifier that you have in there. You're going to have to agree to the terms and conditions. So this is already default checked. And then you're going to give it a name. So you might say website, visitors. Oh, sorry, this is an email one. So all on list. And let's say you're going to give it a date as well. And then you're going to click Next. Facebook will ask you to match the names with the identifiers. And you're going to create your custom audience. And it's that simple. You can now use that custom audience to either exclude from campaigns or run ads towards by using that custom audience as the actual targeting.
